title: moroccan morning ethiopian cocoa gored-gored
description: a unique fusion of moroccan and ethiopian flavors featuring unsweetened cocoa, topped with nuka-pickle.
instructions: in a large bowl, mix together rice flour, injera flour, and cocoa powder.
gradually add in water, stirring until a thick batter forms.
heat olive oil in a large non-stick pan over medium heat.
add a small amount of the batter to the pan and tilt to evenly coat the bottom.
cook for 2-3 minutes, until the edges start to curl.
repeat with the remaining batter, forming small, irregular shapes.
in a separate pan, scramble the eggs over low heat.
once the eggs are fully cooked, chop them into small pieces and set aside.
top each gored-gored shape with a spoonful of nuka-pickle, chopped cilantro, and a sprinkle of cocoa powder.
serve immediately, garnished with additional cilantro if desired.
Note: this recipe is for refrigerator pickling/preserving only — store in the refrigerator and consume within 1 week. Do not water-bath can without an acid (vinegar or lemon juice).
